'''Des Webb''' was the actor who played the [[Wampa/Legends|Wampa]] ice creature on the original [[Star Wars: Episode V The Empire Strikes Back|''Star Wars'': Episode V ''The Empire Strikes Back'']].

==Biography==
Des Webb was not specially familiar to movie acting when he joined the cast of [[Star Wars: Episode V The Empire Strikes Back|''Star Wars'': Episode V ''The Empire Strikes Back'']], having acted in no previous movies.

Webb could not bear the heavy suit and suffered from heat exhaustion. Most of the intended scenes for the "Ice Creature," of which initially there were several, were removed from the final version of the movie. The decision was made that none looked good enough for the final release, and an entire sub-plot about wampas attacking [[Echo Base/Legends|Echo Base]] was removed. Only some labels on doors were retained, and a dead [[Tauntaun/Legends|tauntaun]] being autopsied by [[2-1B/Legends|2-1B]].

Finally, the Wampa almost did not appear on-screen, except for little glimpses. Webb was used for some takes, but the arm knocking [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]] was not Webb's on disguise, and the roaring wampa is just a puppet.

For the [[1997]] re-release of ''Empire Strikes Back'', special effects had advanced and the Wampa could be clearly seen on-screen. This time, there would be no need for a tall actor. [[Industrial Light & Magic|ILM]] artist [[Howie Weed]] designed and wore the new Wampa suit, and new footage would be shot and mixed in with the existing one. Webb was not seen in this new footage.

{{Bio-stub}}
